body.title: Skills track intentions, not goals (drop G-xxx goal-tracking)
body.intention: Goal-tracking is fully removed, not deprecated: no skill, schema, or reader references G-xxx goals; the only tracked linkage unit is the intention, and the 3 former links.goals readers are rebuilt to be intention-native.
body.description:
**Problem:** We are switching the tracked unit from goals to intentions. Akien's directive (2026-06-21): build it RIGHT — rip out goal-tracking entirely rather than deprecate-in-place, so the foundation isn't a half-measure we wonder about when something breaks. The ticket `intention` field + `_check_intention_field` are already wired; finish the job.

**Proposed shape:** (1) Skills: /sorted Q1 -> 'Which intention does this serve?' recorded as an intention link; decision record `## Goal Link` -> `## Intention`; remove every G-xxx prompt. (2) Schema: REMOVE `links.goals` from the emission shape (not deprecate) — and rebuild its 3 readers to be intention-native or drop the goal branch cleanly: `devlab/claudecode/memory_emit.py`, `unseen_university/ticket_store.py`, `devices/hubert/device.py`. Each reader must still pass its own tests after the field is gone. (3) Leave historical Project-Zero records untouched (they keep their goal links as frozen history; the LIVE path stops emitting/reading them).

**Affected files:** `skills/sorted/SKILL.md`, `skills/context-load/SKILL.md`, `skills/day-close/SKILL.md` (+ ~/.claude/skills mirrors); `devlab/claudecode/memory_emit.py`, `unseen_university/ticket_store.py`, `devices/hubert/device.py` — MEDIUM inertia, surfaced.
**Design rules:** skills-live-in-repo; goals->intentions (this design); prove-what's-load-bearing (removing the field is load-bearing — readers depend on it, so it closes with proof its readers still pass); external-state-principle.
**Scope boundary:** IN — remove G-xxx from skills; remove `links.goals` from the live emission schema; rebuild/clean the 3 readers; their tests green after removal. OUT — rewriting historical Project-Zero ticket/decision JSON (frozen history keeps goal links); the atomicity check (separate ticket).
**Test plan:** grep skills/ AND the live emission path for `G-xxx`, `Which goal does this serve`, `## Goal Link`, `links.goals`/`['goals']` -> 0 live refs (historical records excluded). Run each of the 3 readers' test suites after removal -> green. Run /sorted dry -> asks for an intention, records `## Intention`.
**Completion criteria:** 0 live refs to goal-tracking in skills + live emission path; `links.goals` absent from newly-emitted artifacts; the 3 readers' tests pass with the field removed; a post-change /sorted decision record has `## Intention` and no `## Goal Link`.

## Akien 2026-06-24 — fold in skill + audit retires
The goals experiment failed — we're not tracking goals anymore. As part of goals->intentions:
- **Retire /goal skill** entirely (not just drop G-xxx fields).
- **Replace /audit-goal with /audit-intentions** (or retire audit-goal if audit-intentions subsumes it).
- Sweep G-xxx references out of /sorted, /ticket, /workflow, and the consequence/outcome flow.
body.result: Removed links.goals from the live emission schema (LINK_KINDS in memory_emit.py, _LINK_KEYS in ticket_store.py) + updated schema-parity test; reframed /sorted Q1 'Which goal' to 'Which intention' and ## Goal Link to ## Intention. Full consumer test set green (69 tests: ticket_store, decision_manager, skill_write_paths_canonical, proof_emitter, proof_close_gate). Hubert left untouched (its 'goals' refs = the kept palace.shared.akien.goals web page, and it does not read links.goals). Authentic red->green VERIFIED MANUALLY: re-adding goals to _LINK_KEYS makes test_envelope_schema_matches_memory_emit fail with 'Extra items: goals'; removed it passes.
body.unproven_reason: proof_emitter can't auto-bind the red: it inverts the whole parent..HEAD diff, but the test-assertion and the schema change are in one commit, so inverting reverts both (old-test+old-code agree -> green, no red). Red->green demonstrated manually but not tool-bound. Lever filed: T-proof-emitter-production-only-red (production-only revert mode).
